---
title: "TimeBack Alpha Read Packager ‚Äî agent reference"
format: gfm
---

# Agent reference ‚Äî Alpha Read Packager (`arpack`)

Drive `arpack.py` to assemble a **contract-valid Alpha Read course** (OneRoster v1p2 + QTI 3.0)
from QTI items + a skeleton ‚Äî or `course_orchestrator.py` to build a whole course in one command. The
contract is reverse-engineered from a live production course export (1077 items) **and** cross-checked against
Ilma's `incept-timeback-plugin` skill (where we differed, she won ‚Äî corrections applied below).

## The model (verified, do not deviate)
`Course (alpha_read markers + publish flags) ‚Üí flat units ‚Üí resource per lesson (lessonType:"alpha-read-article") ‚Üí QTI assessment-test ‚Üí items + stimulus`.
- Per lesson: **3‚Äì6 guiding** items (1 passage + 1 item each, carries a stimulus-ref) **+ exactly 4 quiz** items (no stimulus). The items we've sampled are 4-option MCQs; the packager accepts any of the 7 formats (it does not require MCQ-ness or a fixed option count beyond a floor of ‚â•2).
- Items: **all 7 r2 formats** handled end-to-end via Ilma's RULE 1 split ‚Äî JSON-safe (`choice`/`order`/single-blank `text-entry`) emit JSON; the rest (`hot-text`/`match`/`ebsr` + multi-blank `text-entry`) ride as raw `<qti-assessment-item>` XML verbatim. (`extended-text` is also routed JSON-safe but unused ‚Äî no fixture emits it.) Answer key from `qti-correct-response`.
- **JSON-safe** items carry the **question stem** in `interaction.questionStructure.prompt` (Ilma's JSON template, create-mcq.md); options nest in `questionStructure.choices`; a stemless JSON item fail-closes. XML-only items carry their stem inside the verbatim XML and are not stem-checked.
- **App ownership (Ramish breaking change, ‚â•2026-06-30):** root `course.primaryApp` is the ONLY ownership setter and must be exactly `"alpha_read"`; `metadata.primaryApp` is now **inert** (mirrored for pre-cutover servers, forward-safe to drop).
- Passage HTML ‚äÜ `div/p/h1/h2/strong/em/br/blockquote` + MathML; **images must be S3 URLs** (no other media). Standards do **not** serialise (live course tags none).

## Run it
1. **Ingest the generator output (parse + normalize, no hand-edits):** `output_dir_ingester.from_timeback_build_output(dir)` reads its `manifest.json` + `items/*.xml` + `stimuli/*.xml` and returns normalized `{item, stimulus}` records (role inferred, format detected, raw XML for XML-only items carried byte-verbatim). Or one item: `arpack.from_qti_xml(xml)` ‚Üí a struct with `identifier, title, role, format, prompt, choices, correct_id(s), rawXml, ‚Ä¶` (`adapt_qti_item` **raises** only for `choice` items whose key isn't among the options; XML-only items are not key-checked).
2. **Ingest the skeleton:** `skeleton_adapter.from_skeleton_table("expeditions.csv")` ‚Üí unit/lesson **shells with no items**.
3. **Materialize ‚Üí assemble ‚Üí validate ‚Üí emit:** the shells must be filled with items first, so use **one command**: `python3 src/course_orchestrator.py skeleton.json out/` (generates per lesson, then assembles + runs the fail-closed validator). `python3 src/arpack.py build` needs an already-materialized skeleton (see `examples/sample_materialized.json`) and rejects bare shells.

## Validator rejects (fail-closed)
non-S3 media ¬∑ MCQ <2 choices ¬∑ key not among choices ¬∑ **missing question stem** (`interaction.questionStructure.prompt`) ¬∑
missing markers/publish flags ¬∑ **root `primaryApp` not exactly `"alpha_read"`** (null/empty/non-string/alias‚Üí422) ¬∑
authored `metadata.metrics` ¬∑ id-coupling breaks ¬∑ wrong section structure ¬∑ content/prompt not well-formed XHTML after sanitize.

## Item checklist ‚Äî what's enforced where
**[pack]** packager `validate()`, fail-closed ¬∑ **[gen]** generation / the jsonl‚ÜíQTI transform (the packager can't see it; InceptBench doesn't check it either) ¬∑ **[probe]** live render in AlphaRead, unconfirmed until the test-student probe.

- **Every item:** [pack] one of the 7 types with correct RULE 1 routing; well-formed XML; root id matches the ref; every `<img>` is an https **S3** URL (no relative paths, no other media). [gen] near-neighbour distractors; Grade-3 Lexile.
- **JSON-safe items also [pack]: a non-empty question stem** (`questionStructure.prompt`). XML-only items are NOT stem-checked ‚Äî the stem lives inside their verbatim XML.
- **MCQ/MSQ (`choice`):** [pack] key ‚äÜ options; exactly 1 key (MCQ) / ‚â•1 (MSQ); ‚â•2 options (the validator floor; live items use 4).
- **Drag-to-order (`order`):** [pack] cardinality `ordered`; key ranks every option.
- **Fill-in (`text-entry`):** [pack] ‚â•1 non-empty accepted answer; single-blank ‚Üí JSON, multi-blank ‚Üí XML with **per-blank** keys (blank N never accepts blank M's answer).
- **Hot-text / match / EBSR (XML-only):** [pack] checks **only** that the envelope is well-formed QTI, has a matching identifier, and is S3-clean ‚Äî it does **not** inspect the answer key or options (RULE 1). Content correctness is therefore [gen]: hot-text's correct token IS the evidence span; match pairs are complete + unambiguous; **EBSR Part B options are verbatim from the passage and depend on Part A**. [probe] all three: does AlphaRead render them?
- **`write_prompt` (Day-2 Read & Write):** 1 MCQ (name the text structure) + 1 hot-text (click the proof) ‚Äî no typing. Both rows above apply; the hot-text [probe] is the load-bearing unknown.
- **Course:** [pack] root `primaryApp:"alpha_read"`; publish flags; 3‚Äì6 guiding + exactly 4 quiz per lesson; resource‚Üîtest id-coupling; no duplicate ids; `lessonType:"alpha-read-article"`.

The headline: the packager guarantees items are **well-formed and shippable**; for hot-text/match/EBSR it does **not** vouch for content correctness ‚Äî that's on generation, and IB doesn't check it.

## Render (live behavior ‚Äî what AlphaRead shows)
Vertical article‚Üíquestion‚Üíunlock. Per lesson: passage + MCQs. The QTI API accepts the tech-enhanced formats,
but **whether AlphaRead renders them is unverified** ‚Äî the open
**renderer probe** (needs a test-student account, not real children). Keep MCQ/MSQ as the safe default.
The reading-course export we sampled is all MCQ, but a MAP-equivalent assessment build in
`trilogy-group/alpha_courses/grade-3-reading/alpha_read_build` already authors hot-text / match / ebsr /
order (sampled, not exhaustively checked) ‚Äî so the all-7 path and the renderer probe gate real content, not just fixtures.

## Cross-check vs Ilma's skill ‚Äî corrections APPLIED
- **D3** component-resource links carry `status:"active"` + `metadata.lessonType` (else 422).
- **G2** every test + item carries `qti-outcome-declaration[SCORE]` (else score always 0).
- **G1** all stimulus/choice/**prompt** content runs through the vendored `sanitize_html.full_sanitize` (XHTML); `validate()` gates on well-formedness. *Highest-value ‚Äî the #1 render bug.*
- **G3** the question **stem** is emitted into `interaction.questionStructure.prompt` (+ options under `questionStructure.choices`) ‚Äî Ilma's JSON template (create-mcq.md / create-frq.md). Previously the parsed prompt was dropped on emit ‚Üí items rendered as naked choices. Now fail-closed.
- **A2** root `primaryApp` is the validator's **hard gate** (exact `"alpha_read"`; null/empty/non-string/alias ‚Üí 422); `metadata.primaryApp` relaxed to **inert/optional** (package valid if dropped; flagged only if present-and-wrong).
- **D4** item-ref `href` = bare `{id}.xml`; **D5** MCQ choice floor relaxed to ‚â•2; resource `url` ‚Üí https.
- **D1/D2 (Ilma RULE 1 ‚Äî IMPLEMENTED):** JSON-safe is exactly `choice` / `extended-text` / `order` / single-blank `text-entry` ‚Üí emitted as JSON. `hottext` / `match` / `associate` / `inline-choice` / `gap-match` / `ebsr` / `select-point` **and multi-blank `text-entry`** ‚Üí emitted as the raw-XML envelope `{"format":"xml","xml":<rawXml>}`, carried byte-verbatim (the JSON‚ÜíXML converter corrupts their scoring otherwise). The invented `map_response` template is gone (Ilma uses `match_correct`). The split is decided **per item** (`_is_xml_only`); `validate()` checks XML-only items as a well-formed envelope, not as a JSON option model.

## Upload ‚Äî two real targets
`arpack` is a clean offline emitter; the upload is a separate step. Two proven/known surfaces:

**Demo (proven, no real creds):** the [platform3 content-alpha](https://platform3-andymontgomery-9773s-projects.vercel.app/content/alpha/implementation/api)
`demo` tenant. `POST /dev/mint?tenantId=demo` ‚Üí token; `POST /tenants/demo/alpha/content/imports/qti-package`
with `Content-Type: application/xml` + an `Idempotency-Key` header + the raw item XML body (the surface parses
it ‚Äî its rule: do NOT parse QTI locally). Read back `‚Ä¶/items/{id}/trust` (expect `trust_status:"trusted"`) and
`‚Ä¶/student-view`. This is a real TimeBack Content surface (its QC trusts our generated hot-text/match/ebsr),
but a sandbox tenant ‚Äî NOT the live Alpha Read app, so it does not answer the render question.

**Live Alpha Read (production):** push via **Ilma's `/timeback` skill**
([incept-timeback-plugin](https://github.com/ilmych/incept-timeback-plugin)), NOT hand-rolled ‚Äî she owns auth
(Cognito body creds + token cache + 401 refresh), the push order (S3‚Üícourse‚Üíunits‚Üílessons‚Üíitems‚Üítests‚Üístimuli‚Üí
resources(trailing `/`)‚Üílinks, 409=success, checkpoint each), JSON-vs-XML branching, and GET-modify-PUT.
Pre-flight: write-scoped Cognito client (via gitignored `.env`) + `POST /validate`. Push only to a
`STAN-PROBE-DELETEME‚Ä¶` draft; post-push, open ‚â•1 lesson in the student UI (200 ‚âÝ rendered).
**Done once (2026-06-17):** items POST verbatim as `{"format":"xml","xml":<raw>}` to `‚Ä¶/api/assessment-items`;
test ‚Üí `‚Ä¶/api/assessment-tests`; stimuli ‚Üí `‚Ä¶/api/stimuli` (`{identifier,title,content}`); OneRoster course/
components/resources(`/resources/v1p2/resources/`)/links under `‚Ä¶/rostering/v1p2/...`. **Org gotcha:** a draft
must be created in an org the viewer belongs to ‚Äî the documented example org returned "Access denied" in
AlphaBuild; using the viewer's own org rendered fine. **Automated** as `examples/push_to_timeback.py`
(idempotent ¬∑ checkpointed ¬∑ 409=success ¬∑ draft-guarded ¬∑ `--verify` read-back): e.g.
`python3 examples/push_to_timeback.py --package qti_package/ --org <viewer-org-sourcedId> --verify
[--enroll-student <user-sourcedId>]`, creds from a gitignored `.env`. `--enroll-student` adds the
term‚Üíclass‚Üíenrollment so the course surfaces in the student app (use a TEST student you own; never a real
child ‚Äî and the student LOGIN is a human step, not automatable). The OneRoster class needs `org` (not just
`school`) and its `terms` must be in the same org (so the tool mints a draft term in `--org`).
**Alpha Read render gotcha (the #1 student-app 404):** a resource's `vendorResourceId` must be the BARE
NUMBER `<N>` and its QTI test must live at id `article_<N>` ‚Äî the student app fetches
`assessment-tests/article_<vendorResourceId>`. Using the test id (non-numeric) as `vendorResourceId` ‚Üí
"article does not exist". The tool now emits `test=article_<N>`, `vendorResourceId=<N>`. **The OneRoster resource AND component-resource
sourcedIds must ALSO be `article_<N>`** (same as the test): the Alpha Read DASHBOARD derives each article's
link from them ‚Äî with `-res`/`-link` ids it builds a link with no `articleId` ‚Üí 404, even though the direct
`?articleId=<N>&crsid=article_<N>` URL works. So test = resource = component-resource = `article_<N>`. The
student-app URL is `https://alpharead.alpha-1edtech.ai/articles?articleId=<N>&crsid=article_<N>`.
**Verified 2026-06-17:** clicking the course in the dashboard renders the real ELA article end-to-end
(passage ‚Üí guiding-question unlock flow, 25-step reading session, step types matching the live article).

## Verified vs open
- **Verified (offline):** ingests the real `incept-qti-sdk` output with no hand-edits (the 4-item + 13-item all-7 fixtures); parses every item of a live course export we pulled (~1077) cleanly (no wrong keys/blanks); emits a graph whose contract-invariant fields match that export (checked by the parity tests on the course header + one round-tripped lesson); the one-command orchestrator (**stub** generator) ‚Üí package passes `validate`; sanitizer + Phase-1 corrections applied; cross-checked against Ilma's skill (her corrections adopted where we differed).
- **Server-validated (demo tenant):** the QTI for all 6 sample formats (incl. raw-XML hot-text/match/ebsr) imports QC-trusted to the platform3 content-alpha `demo` tenant via `qti-package` ‚Äî `trust_status:"trusted"`, `published`, student-view with answer keys hidden. Confirmed for both the bridge-generated QTI AND the production `incept-qti-sdk` package (6 items, real ELA sample), which our packager also parses clean. Real TimeBack QC, sandbox tenant ‚Äî not the live Alpha Read renderer (that's the cred-gated live push).
- **Live push + render (verified 2026-06-17):** the production package (6 items + 6 stimuli) pushed to live Alpha Read as a `STAN-PROBE-DELETEME` draft (all 201) and **renders in AlphaBuild** ‚Äî all 6 formats present, QTI panel **Valid**, live preview shows the passage + question.
- **Student render wired (2026-06-17):** a test student (own profile, `student` role) enrolled via term‚Üíclass‚Üíenrollment; the course surfaces in the student's class list, and AlphaBuild's "Preview (from Timeback) **LIVE**" renders the real two-pane reading view (passage + question + selectable options). The only non-automatable step is the student **login** (agents must not create accounts or authenticate as students).
- **Open:** a full multi-lesson course end-to-end awaits the live skeleton + full generator.
